examples.da: Jeg skal på ferie i næste uge.
examples.en: I am going on vacation next week.

examples.da: Der er syv dage i en uge.
examples.en: There are seven days in a week.

examples.da: Vi mødes klokken seks i morgen.
examples.en: We meet at six o'clock tomorrow.
She has six siblings.

seks.gotcha: Pronounced the same as "sex" — context tells them apart.

examples.da: Må jeg spørge dig om noget?
examples.en: May I ask you something?
You can always ask the teacher.

spørge.gotcha: Irregular past: spurgte, spurgt. Use "bede om" to ask FOR something; spørge is asking a question.

examples.en: She held the child by the hand.
Give me your hand.
My sister lives in Copenhagen.
Do you have a big sister?
My grandmother is still alive.

examples.da: De lever af at sælge fisk.
examples.en: They make a living selling fish.

leve.gotcha: "Leve" is to be alive; "bo" is to live somewhere. Don't say "jeg lever i København".

examples.da: Er der plads til mig i bilen?
examples.en: Is there room for me in the car?

examples.da: Vi mødes på pladsen foran kirken.
examples.en: We meet in the square in front of the church.

plads.gotcha: Also means "room/space" (uncountable) and "square" in a town — not just a seat.

examples.da: Hun arbejder som hjælper på et plejehjem.
examples.en: She works as a helper at a nursing home.

hjælper.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "hjælpe" (helps) — context decides.

examples.da: Han reddede barnet fra at drukne.
examples.en: He saved the child from drowning.
We must save the environment.

redde.gotcha: Don't confuse with "rede" (to make a bed; nest). Soft d in both.

examples.da: Det er sgu en god idé!
examples.en: That's a damn good idea!
I honestly don't know.

sgu.gotcha: Mildly vulgar (from "så gud"). Very common in speech, but avoid in formal or polite settings.

examples.en: Halt! Not one more step.

holdt.gotcha: Same form as the past tense of "holde" (held) — context decides.

examples.en: Tell me a story.
She studies history at university.

examples.da: Sine kommer til festen i aften.
examples.en: Sine is coming to the party tonight.

Sine.gotcha: Looks like the pronoun "sine" (his/her own, plural) — capitalization and context decide.

examples.en: He broke his leg on the ski trip.
The dog is chewing on a bone.

ben.gotcha: Means both "leg" and "bone". Plural is also "ben" (unchanged).

examples.en: The website has many new users.

bruger.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "bruge" (uses) — context decides.

examples.da: Jeg vil helst blive hjemme i dag.
examples.en: I would rather stay home today.
Preferably come before nine o'clock.

helst.gotcha: "Vil helst" = would prefer to. Also in "hvad som helst" (anything at all).

examples.da: Hun har et fast job nu.
examples.en: She has a permanent job now.
Hold on tight to the railing.

fast.gotcha: False friend: not English "fast" (quick) — that is "hurtig". Means firm, fixed, permanent.

examples.da: Der var blod på hans skjorte.
examples.en: There was blood on his shirt.

examples.da: Koncerten bliver sendt live på TV.
examples.en: The concert is broadcast live on TV.
The music livened up the party.

live.gotcha: Don't confuse with "leve" (to live) or the phrase "i live" (alive).

examples.en: What is your phone number?

examples.da: Det er mit yndlingsnummer på albummet.
examples.en: It's my favorite track on the album.

examples.da: Vil du have en kop the?
examples.en: Would you like a cup of tea?

the.gotcha: False friend: not English "the" — it's a spelling of "te" (tea). Danish has no word for "the"; it uses endings.

examples.da: Min kæreste og jeg skal ud at rejse.
examples.en: My girlfriend and I are going traveling.
Do you have a boyfriend or girlfriend?

kæreste.gotcha: Gender-neutral: means boyfriend OR girlfriend. Also the superlative of "kær" (dearest).

examples.en: The Earth revolves around the sun.

Jorden.gotcha: Capitalized for the planet; lowercase "jorden" means the ground or soil.

examples.en: The apple fell to the ground.

examples.da: Han lå på jorden og hvilede sig.
examples.en: He lay on the ground resting.

jorden.gotcha: Definite form of "jord" (soil, ground). Capitalized "Jorden" is the planet Earth.

examples.en: The course is for beginners.

begynder.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "begynde" (begins) — context decides.

examples.da: Skal vi gå en tur i parken?
examples.en: Shall we take a walk in the park?
Now it's your turn.

tur.gotcha: Means both a trip/walk and a turn (in a game) — context decides.

examples.en: The children are playing football in the garden.
She plays the piano every day.

spille.gotcha: Use "spille" for games and instruments, but "lege" for children playing freely.

examples.en: I slept badly last night.
Good night and sleep well!

nat.gotcha: Irregular plural: nætter. "I nat" can mean last night or tonight, depending on context.

examples.da: Jeg går i seng klokken elleve.
examples.en: I go to bed at eleven o'clock.

examples.da: Sengen står i det lille værelse.
examples.en: The bed is in the small room.

examples.da: Du har truffet et godt valg.
examples.en: You have made a good choice.

examples.da: Der er valg i Danmark til november.
examples.en: There is an election in Denmark in November.

examples.da: De har været gift i ti år.
examples.en: They have been married for ten years.
The bottle contains poison.

gift.gotcha: Famous trap: means both "married" and "poison". Not English "gift" — a present is "gave".

examples.da: Det er skide koldt i dag.
examples.en: It's bloody cold today.
He couldn't care less about the rules.

skide.gotcha: Vulgar. As an adverb it just intensifies ("skide godt" = damn good) — informal speech only.

examples.en: The glass is completely full.
He got drunk at the party.

fuld.gotcha: Trap: about people it usually means "drunk", not "full". "Jeg er mæt" = I'm full (of food).

examples.en: The movie lasts two hours.
We have Danish class at ten o'clock.

time.gotcha: False friend: not English "time" (that is "tid"). Means hour or a school lesson.

examples.da: Det regnede, men vi gik alligevel.
examples.en: It was raining, but we went anyway.

examples.da: Jeg vil gerne lære at svømme.
examples.en: I would like to learn to swim.
She teaches us Danish.

lære.gotcha: Means both "to learn" and "to teach" — context decides. A teacher is "en lærer".

examples.da: Det var en rigtig god idé.
examples.en: That was a really good idea.

examples.da: Har du nogen idéer til gaven?
examples.en: Do you have any ideas for the gift?

idé.gotcha: Stress on the last syllable: i-DÉ.

examples.en: Here you are, here is your coffee.
Please have a seat.

værsgo.gotcha: From "vær så god". Used when handing something over or inviting — no exact English equivalent.

examples.da: Må jeg få et glas vand?
examples.en: May I have a glass of water?
The water in the lake is cold.

vand.gotcha: Silent d: pronounced "van'". Colloquially "en vand" can mean a soft drink.

examples.da: Han er den bedste spiller på holdet.
examples.en: He is the best player on the team.

spiller.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "spille" (plays) — context decides.

examples.da: Det er kun et tænkt eksempel.
examples.en: It is only a hypothetical example.

examples.da: Har du tænkt over mit forslag?
examples.en: Have you thought about my suggestion?
She has short hair now.
Can you pay by card?

kort.gotcha: As a noun (et kort) it means card or map; as an adjective, "short". Plural of the noun is unchanged.

examples.da: Min lejlighed er mindre end din.
examples.en: My apartment is smaller than yours.

examples.da: Vi bruger mindre tid på transport nu.
examples.en: We spend less time on transport now.

mindre.gotcha: Comparative of both "lille" (smaller) and "lidt" (less) — context decides.

examples.da: Du har fået en besked på telefonen.
examples.en: You have received a message on your phone.

examples.da: Giv mig besked, når du kommer hjem.
examples.en: Let me know when you get home.

besked.gotcha: "Give besked" means to notify, not to give an order.

examples.en: The train leaves at exactly eight o'clock.

examples.da: Præcis! Det var det, jeg mente.
examples.en: Exactly! That's what I meant.

examples.da: Jeg har ondt i læggen efter løbeturen.
examples.en: My calf hurts after the run.

læg.gotcha: Same form as the imperative of "lægge" (put/lay down!) — context decides.

examples.da: Der var en masse mennesker i byen.
examples.en: There were a lot of people in town.
We have plenty of time.

masse.gotcha: "En masse" is a very common way to say "a lot of".

examples.en: She has beautiful blue eyes.

examples.da: Han øjnede en chance for at vinde.
examples.en: He glimpsed a chance to win.

øjne.gotcha: Usually the irregular plural of "øje" (eye); the verb "at øjne" is rarer.

examples.en: What time is it?
We eat at seven o'clock.

klokken.gotcha: "Klokken" comes BEFORE the number: "klokken syv" = seven o'clock. Literally "the bell/clock".

examples.da: Du har fået et forkert nummer.
examples.en: You have got a wrong number.
I answered the question incorrectly.

examples.da: Der er meget fedt i smør.
examples.en: There is a lot of fat in butter.

examples.da: Hvor er det fedt, at du kommer!
examples.en: How cool that you are coming!

fedt.gotcha: As slang, "fedt" means "cool/great" — very common and positive despite meaning "fat".

examples.en: He works as a secret agent.
What a beautiful sunset!
She sang a beautiful song.

smuk.gotcha: Neuter form is "smukt", plural "smukke" (doubles the k).

examples.en: The captain is on the bridge.
She is the captain of the team.

examples.da: Jeg har fået noget i øjet.
examples.en: I have got something in my eye.
Keep an eye on the bag.

øje.gotcha: Irregular plural: øjne. Also et-word despite ending in -e.

examples.da: Jeg forstår ikke, hvad du siger.
examples.en: I don't understand what you are saying.
Can you understand Danish?

examples.da: Han skrev et brev til sin elskede.
examples.en: He wrote a letter to his beloved.

elskede.gotcha: Same form as the past tense of "elske" (loved) — context decides.

examples.en: The movie is over at ten o'clock.
It's over between us.

slut.gotcha: False friend: nothing to do with the English insult. Just means "finished/the end".

examples.en: My parents live in Aarhus.

examples.da: Hun besøger sine forældre hver søndag.
examples.en: She visits her parents every Sunday.

forældre.gotcha: Plural only — a single parent is "en forælder".

examples.da: Må jeg få lov til at prøve?
examples.en: May I be allowed to try?

examples.da: Folketinget har vedtaget en ny lov.
examples.en: Parliament has passed a new law.

lov.gotcha: Means both "law" and "permission": "få lov til" = be allowed to. Silent v tendency: often "lov'".

examples.en: Put the cup on the table.
We sat down by the window.

sætte.gotcha: Irregular past: satte, sat. "Sætte sig" = sit down; don't confuse with "sidde" (be sitting).

examples.da: Det er ikke muligt at nå toget nu.
examples.en: It is not possible to catch the train now.
Come as quickly as possible.

examples.da: Kærlighed er det vigtigste i livet.
examples.en: Love is the most important thing in life.

examples.da: Det var kærlighed ved første blik.
examples.en: It was love at first sight.
The liver cleans the blood in the body.

lever.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "leve" (lives) — context decides.

examples.da: Hvad er din mening om filmen?
examples.en: What is your opinion of the movie?
It makes no sense.

mening.gotcha: False friend: means "opinion" as often as "meaning". "Efter min mening" = in my opinion.

examples.da: Jeg har en tid hos dr. Hansen.
examples.en: I have an appointment with Dr. Hansen.

dr..gotcha: Also abbreviates "dronning" (queen), as in "dr. Margrethe" — and DR is the Danish broadcaster.

examples.en: My heart is beating fast.
She has a good heart.
My boss is very nice.

examples.da: Jeg skal tale med chefen i morgen.
examples.en: I have to talk to the boss tomorrow.

chef.gotcha: False friend: means "boss", not "chef" (a cook is "kok").

examples.en: The car is parked in front of the house.
She stood at the front of the queue.

examples.da: Jeg er ligeglad med, hvad de siger.
examples.en: I don't care what they say.
He seems completely indifferent.

ligeglad.gotcha: Takes the preposition "med": "ligeglad med" = don't care about. Can sound rude.

examples.en: The party was really fun.
He always tells funny stories.

sjov.gotcha: "For sjov" = for fun, as a joke. Covers both "fun" and "funny".

examples.da: Vejret er perfekt til en strandtur.
examples.en: The weather is perfect for a beach trip.
They have two small children.

examples.da: Der er små ti minutters gang.
examples.en: It is just under a ten-minute walk.

små.gotcha: Irregular plural of "lille" — "lille" has no regular plural form.

examples.en: Close the door, there's a draft.
Open up, I'm freezing!

luk.gotcha: "Luk op" means open (!), while "luk" alone means close. "Luk i/luk røven" = shut up (rude).

examples.en: The police are investigating the case.
That is not my business.

examples.da: På en vis måde har du ret.
examples.en: In a certain way you are right.

examples.da: Han er en klog og vis mand.
examples.en: He is a smart and wise man.

vis.gotcha: Two adjectives in one form: "certain" and "wise". Also imperative of "vise" (show!).

examples.da: Jeg har lyst til en is.
examples.en: I feel like having an ice cream.

examples.da: Har du lyst til at komme med?
examples.en: Do you feel like coming along?

lyst.gotcha: "Have lyst til" = feel like/want to — everyday phrase, no sexual meaning. Also neuter of "lys" (light).

examples.da: Den store viser står på tolv.
examples.en: The big hand points to twelve.

viser.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "vise" (shows) — context decides.

examples.en: Leave me alone!
The countries made peace after the war.

fred.gotcha: Soft d: pronounced "freð". "I fred" = in peace/alone.

examples.en: The weather will get worse tomorrow.

examples.da: Det kunne have været meget værre.
examples.en: It could have been much worse.

examples.da: Vi har en aftale klokken tre.
examples.en: We have an appointment at three o'clock.
Shall we agree on a time?

aftale.gotcha: Covers both "appointment" and "agreement/deal". "Det er en aftale!" = it's a deal!

examples.en: She finished last.
Thanks for last time!

sidst.gotcha: "Tak for sidst" is a fixed greeting after meeting someone — no English equivalent.

examples.en: Tom is my best friend.
The bottle is empty.

examples.da: Huset stod tomt i flere år.
examples.en: The house stood empty for several years.

tom.gotcha: Neuter form is "tomt", plural "tomme" (doubles the m).

examples.da: Hvad er planen for i aften?
examples.en: What is the plan for tonight?

examples.da: Vi lagde en plan for rejsen.
examples.en: We made a plan for the trip.

examples.da: Vi skal ud på en lang rejse.
examples.en: We are going on a long journey.

examples.da: Hun rejser til Spanien i morgen.
examples.en: She is traveling to Spain tomorrow.

rejse.gotcha: Also means "to raise": "rejse sig" = to stand up.

examples.en: The children are playing outside.

examples.da: Han følte sig udenfor i klassen.
examples.en: He felt left out in class.
I pick up the children at four o'clock.

examples.da: Kan du hente mælk på vejen hjem?
examples.en: Can you get milk on the way home?

hente.gotcha: Also the standard word for "to download" an app or file.

examples.da: Jeg venter stadig på et svar.
examples.en: I am still waiting for an answer.
She gave a quick answer.

svar.gotcha: Plural is unchanged: et svar, flere svar.

examples.en: The police are looking for the killer.

dræber.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "dræbe" (kills) — context decides.

examples.en: The cat has had three kittens.

examples.da: De unge hænger ud i parken.
examples.en: The young people hang out in the park.

unge.gotcha: "En unge" (animal young/kid) vs. "de unge" (young people, from adjective "ung") — context decides.

examples.da: Hun havde et stort smil på ansigtet.
examples.en: She had a big smile on her face.
Wash your face before breakfast.
Aw, how cute it is!

examples.da: Om du vil eller ej, skal du med.
examples.en: Whether you want to or not, you are coming.

ej.gotcha: As an interjection it expresses surprise or mild protest; as "or not" it appears in "eller ej".

examples.en: Good luck with the exam!

examples.da: Vi vandt kun ved rent held.
examples.en: We only won by pure luck.

held.gotcha: False friend: not English "held" (past of hold). Silent d.

examples.da: Jeg skal købe ind til weekenden.
examples.en: I have to shop for the weekend.
She bought a new bicycle.

købe.gotcha: Pronounced roughly "kø-be" with soft b; don't confuse with "koge" (to boil).

examples.en: The book is worth reading.
How much is the house worth?

værd.gotcha: Only used predicatively, after what it describes: "det er pengene værd" = it's worth the money.

examples.en: Can I pay by card?
Who is paying for dinner?
I slept badly last night.

examples.da: Hun har det dårligt i dag.
examples.en: She is feeling unwell today.

dårligt.gotcha: "Have det dårligt" = feel unwell; "jeg er dårlig" often means "I am sick".

examples.en: Finally summer arrived!
It is the final decision.

endelig.gotcha: In requests, "endelig" means "by all means": "kom endelig!" = do come!

examples.en: Sunscreen protects the skin from the sun.
We must protect nature.
Is there life on Mars?

examples.da: Den slags butikker findes ikke længere.
examples.en: That kind of shop no longer exists.

findes.gotcha: A passive-form verb (s-form) used actively — very common for "there is/exists".

examples.da: Skal vi spille et spil kort?
examples.en: Shall we play a game of cards?
Computer games are popular among young people.

spil.gotcha: Plural is unchanged: et spil, flere spil. Also imperative of "spille" (play!).

examples.en: The bag is easy to carry.
The task was very easy.

let.gotcha: Means both "light" (not heavy) and "easy" — false friend with English "let" (allow).

examples.da: Radioens sender står på en bakke.
examples.en: The radio's transmitter stands on a hill.

sender.gotcha: Same form as the present tense of "sende" (sends) — context decides.

examples.da: Mit værelse er på første sal.
examples.en: My room is on the first floor.
The hotel has a hundred rooms.

værelse.gotcha: A room in a home/hotel; a room as "space" is "plads", and "rum" is more general.

examples.en: The company had to fire ten employees.

examples.da: Vi fyrer op i pejsen i aften.
examples.en: We are lighting the fireplace tonight.

fyre.gotcha: Also the plural of "fyr" (guy): "to fyre" = two guys.

examples.en: The movie was really bad.

examples.da: Jeg er dårlig til at synge.
examples.en: I am bad at singing.

dårlig.gotcha: About a person it often means "ill": "jeg er dårlig" = I feel sick.

examples.en: May I ask for the salt?

examples.da: Hun beder til Gud hver aften.
examples.en: She prays to God every evening.

bede.gotcha: Irregular past: bad, bedt. "Bede om" = ask FOR something; "spørge" = ask a question.

examples.en: Denmark is a small country.
They live in the countryside.

land.gotcha: "På landet" = in the countryside; "i landet" = in the country (nation). Silent d.

examples.da: Det er koldt derude i dag.
examples.en: It is cold out there today.
Who is out there?

examples.da: Er du stadig oppe? Det er midnat.
examples.en: Are you still up? It's midnight.

examples.da: Han bor oppe på anden sal.
examples.en: He lives up on the second floor.

oppe.gotcha: "Oppe" is location (being up); "op" is movement (going up). Danish splits these strictly.

examples.en: I have a headache.
Use your head!

hoved.gotcha: Pronounced "ho'ed" — the v is silent. Irregular plural: hoveder.

examples.da: Der er plads til fire personer i bilen.
examples.en: There is room for four people in the car.

examples.da: Hun er en meget venlig person.
examples.en: She is a very friendly person.

person.gotcha: Stress on the last syllable: per-SON.

examples.da: Må jeg få et stykke kage?
examples.en: May I have a piece of cake?

examples.da: Vi gik et stykke ned ad vejen.
examples.en: We walked a bit down the road.

stykke.gotcha: "Et stykke tid" = a while. Abbreviated "stk." on price tags (per item).

examples.da: Jeg er træt og vil i seng.
examples.en: I am tired and want to go to bed.

examples.da: Hun er træt af at vente.
examples.en: She is tired of waiting.

træt.gotcha: "Træt af" = fed up with. Don't confuse with "træ" (tree/wood).

examples.en: Aarhus is Denmark's second largest city.

examples.da: Skal vi i byen i aften?
examples.en: Shall we go out on the town tonight?

by.gotcha: False friend: not English "by". "I byen" often means going out (bars, shopping).

examples.en: They talked openly about sex.

sex.gotcha: Pronounced the same as "seks" (six) — context tells them apart.

examples.en: What lovely weather!
What a lot of people there are!

sikke.gotcha: Exclamation only: "sikke en/et..." = what a...! Not the question word "hvad".

examples.da: Kære Anna, tak for dit brev.
examples.en: Dear Anna, thank you for your letter.

kære.gotcha: Most common as "dear" (from adjective "kær"), e.g. starting letters; the legal verb "to appeal" is rare.

examples.en: Safety comes first in the workplace.

examples.da: Jeg kan ikke sige det med sikkerhed.
examples.en: I cannot say it with certainty.

sikkerhed.gotcha: Covers both "safety" and "security" — Danish does not distinguish.

examples.en: Shall we meet in front of the cinema?

examples.da: De mødes hver fredag til kaffe.
examples.en: They meet every Friday for coffee.

mødes.gotcha: Reciprocal s-form of "møde": "vi mødes" = we meet each other.

examples.da: Jeg kunne ikke sove i nat.
examples.en: I could not sleep last night.
The baby is finally sleeping.

sove.gotcha: Irregular past: sov, sovet. Don't confuse "sove" (be asleep) with "falde i søvn" (fall asleep).

examples.da: Hvad ønsker du dig i fødselsdagsgave?
examples.en: What do you wish for as a birthday present?

examples.da: Mit største ønske er at rejse jorden rundt.
examples.en: My biggest wish is to travel around the world.

ønske.gotcha: "Ønske sig" (with sig) = wish for something for oneself, e.g. gifts.

examples.en: You idiot, you forgot the keys!

idiot.gotcha: Stress on the last syllable: idi-OT. "Din idiot!" = you idiot (uses "din", not "du").

examples.en: He broke his arm in the fall.

examples.da: I så fald kommer jeg i morgen.
examples.en: In that case I will come tomorrow.

fald.gotcha: Common in fixed phrases: "i hvert fald" = in any case/at least. Silent d.
